text:
Unprecedented image quality in holographic near-eye displays with a fast phase-only SLM 

Holographic near-eye displays offer unprecedented capabilities for virtual and augmented reality systems, including perceptually important focus cues. Although artificial intelligence--driven algorithms for computer-generated holography (CGH) have recently made much progress in improving the image quality and synthesis efficiency of holograms, these algorithms are not directly applicable to emerging phase-only spatial light modulators (SLM) that are extremely fast but offer phase control with very limited precision. The speed of these SLMs offers time multiplexing capabilities, essentially enabling partially-coherent holographic display modes. Here we report advances in camera-calibrated wave propagation models for these types of near-eye holographic displays and we develop a CGH framework that robustly optimizes the heavily quantized phase patterns of fast SLMs. Our framework is flexible in supporting runtime supervision with different types of content, including 2D and 2.5D RGBD images, 3D focal stacks, and 4D light fields. Using our framework, we demonstrate state-of-the-art results for all of these scenarios in simulation and experiment.

Please check the project page for the paper and more videos.

text: In the context of deep learning for robotics, we show effective method of training a real robot to grasp a tiny sphere (1:37cm of diameter), with an original combination of system design choices. We decompose the end-to-end system into a vision module and a closed-loop controller module. The two modules use target object segmentation as their common interface. The vision module extracts information from the robot end-effector camera, in the form of a binary segmentation mask of the target. We train it to achieve effective domain transfer by composing real background images with simulated images of the target. The controller module takes as input the binary segmentation mask, and thus is agnostic to visual discrepancies between simulated and real environments. We train our closed-loop controller in simulation using imitation learning and show it is robust with respect to discrepancies between the dynamic model of the simulated and real robot: when combined with eye-in-hand observations, we achieve a 90% success rate in grasping a tiny sphere with a real robot. The controller can generalize to unseen scenarios where the target is moving and even learns to recover from failures.

text: The proliferation of 3D representations, from explicit meshes to implicit neural fields and more, motivates the need for simulators agnostic to representation. We present a data-, mesh-, and grid-free solution for elastic simulation for any object in any geometric representation undergoing large, nonlinear deformations. We note that every standard geometric representation can be reduced to an occupancy function queried at any point in space, and we define a simulator atop this common interface. For each object, we fit a small implicit neural network encoding spatially varying weights that act as a reduced deformation basis. These weights are trained to learn physically significant motions in the object via random perturbations. Our loss ensures we find a weight-space basis that best minimizes deformation energy by stochastically evaluating elastic energies through Monte Carlo sampling of the deformation volume.At runtime, we simulate in the reduced basis and sample the deformations back to the original domain.Our experiments demonstrate the versatility, accuracy, and speed of this approach on data including signed distance functions, point clouds, neural primitives, tomography scans, radiance fields, Gaussian splats, surface meshes, and volume meshes, as well as showing a variety of material energies, contact models, and time integration schemes.

text: Vectored IR drop analysis is a critical step in chip signoff that checks the power integrity of an on-chip power delivery network. Due to the prohibitive runtimes of dynamic IR drop analysis, the large number of test patterns must be whittled down to a small subset of worstcase IR vectors. Unlike the traditional slow heuristic method that select a few vectors with incomplete coverage, MAVIREC uses machine learning techniques—3D convolutions and regression-like layers—for accurately recommending a larger subset of test patterns that exercise worst-case scenarios. In under 30 minutes, MAVIREC profiles 100K-cycle vectors and provides better coverage than a state-of-the-art industrial flow. Further, MAVIREC’s IR drop predictor shows 10X speedup with under 4mV RMSE relative to an industrial flow.

text: Many applications ranging from quasi-Monte Carlo integration over optimal control to neural networks benefit from high-dimensional, highly uniform samples. In the case of computer graphics,  and more particularly in rendering, despite the need for uniformity, several sub-problems expose a low-dimensional structure. In this context, mastering sampling uniformity over projections while preserving high-dimensional uniformity has been intrinsically challenging. This difficulty may explain the relatively small number of mathematical constructions for such samplers. We propose a novel approach by showing that uniformity constraints can be expressed as an integer linear program that results in a sampler with the desired properties. As it turns out, complex constraints are easy to describe by means of stratification and sequence properties of digital nets. Formalized using generator matrix determinants, our new MatBuilder software solves the set of constraints by iterating the linear integer program solver in a greedy fashion to compute a problem-specific set of generator matrices that can be used as a drop-in replacement in the popular digital net samplers. The samplers created by MatBuilder achieve the uniformity of classic low discrepancy sequences. More importantly, we demonstrate the benefit of the unprecedented versatility of our constraint approach with respect to low-dimensional problem structure for several applications.

Overview



The Ultimate Single-Slot GPU for AI, Neural Rendering, and Precision Workflows


Experience the power of the most advanced single-slot GPU for professionals. Powered by NVIDIA Blackwell architecture, NVIDIA RTX PRO 4000 Blackwell delivers breakthrough AI performance, neural graphics, and unmatched efficiency to unlock new experiences and accelerate demanding workflows. Equipped with 24GB of ultra-fast memory, fifth-generation Tensor Cores, and fourth-generation RT Cores, work with large datasets, accelerate generative AI workflows, and render photorealistic scenes at blazing speeds.
NVIDIA Blackwell RTX PRO Comes to Workstations and Servers for Designers, Developers, Data Scientists and Creatives to Build and Collaborate With Agentic AI

Groundbreaking AI and graphics performance redefine visualization, simulation and scientific computing from desktop to data center for millions of professionals worldwide.
Read Press Release


Highlights



Industry-Leading Performance


AI Performance

Features



Powered by NVIDIA Blackwell Architecture


NVIDIA Blackwell Architecture

Fifth-Gen Tensor Cores


Max AI performance with FP4 and DLSS 4

New Streaming Multiprocessors


Optimized for RTX Neural Shaders

Fourth-Gen Ray Tracing Cores


Built for RTX Mega Geometry

5th Gen Tensor Cores
5th Gen Tensor Cores

Fifth-generation Tensor Cores deliver up to 3X the performance of the previous generation and add support for FP4 precision and DLSS 4 Multi Frame Generation technology. Accelerate local LLMs, prototype new AI models, and drive enhanced content creation and graphics.
4th Gen RT Cores
4th Gen RT Cores

Fourth-generation RT Cores deliver up to 2X the performance of the previous generation, accelerating rendering for M&E content creation, AECO design, and manufacturing prototyping. Create photoreal, physically accurate scenes and immersive 3D designs with neural graphics-based technologies, such as RTX Mega Geometry, enabling up to 100X more ray-traced triangles.
CUDA Cores
CUDA Cores

NVIDIA Blackwell is the most powerful professional RTX GPU ever created with the latest SM and CUDA core technology. The SM features increased processing throughput, and new neural shaders that integrate neural networks inside of programmable shaders to drive the next decade of AI-augmented graphics innovations.
24 GB of GPU Memory
24GB of GPU Memory

New and improved GDDR7 memory significantly boosts bandwidth and capacity, empowering your applications to run faster, and work with larger, more complex datasets. With 24GB of GPU memory, tackle large 3D and AI projects, explore immersive VR environments, and drive multi-app workflows.
9th-Gen NVENC
9th-Gen NVENC

Ninth-generation NVIDIA NVENC engines significantly accelerate video encoding speed, and improve quality for professional video applications. They add support for 4:2:2 H.264 and HEVC encoding, and improve HEVC and AV1 encoding quality.
6th-Gen NVDEC
6th Gen NVDEC

Sixth-generation NVIDIA NVDEC engines provide up to double H.264 decoding throughput and offer support for 4:2:2 H.264 and HEVC decode. Professionals can benefit from high-quality video playback, accelerate video data ingestion, and use advanced AI-powered video editing features.
PCIe Gen 5
PCIe Gen 5

Support for PCI Express Gen 5 provides double the bandwidth of PCIe Gen 4, improving data-transfer speeds from CPU memory and unlocking faster performance for data-intensive tasks like AI, data science, and 3D modeling.
DisplayPort 2.1
DisplayPort 2.1

With DisplayPort 2.1, professionals can achieve unparalleled visual clarity and performance, driving high-resolution displays at up to 8K at 240Hz and 16K at 60Hz. Increased bandwidth enables seamless multi-monitor setups, ideal for multitasking and collaboration, while HDR and higher color depth support ensures superior color accuracy for precision work, such as video editing, 3D design, and live broadcasting.

Workloads



Multi-Workload Acceleration


Generative AI

Create new and original content.

Unlock unprecedented creativity and productivity with generative AI. The RTX PRO 4000 accelerates content creation, empowering you to generate stunning, high-fidelity images, videos, and 3D assets at unprecedented speeds. Experience accelerated inference performance in generative AI applications to bring your most ambitious ideas to life in record time, changing the boundaries of entertainment, design, and innovation across industries.
Learn More About Generative AI
Unlock unprecedented creativity and productivity with generative AI
AI-Driven Rendering and Graphics

Deliver stunningly realistic designs and simulations with speed and precision.

The RTX PRO 4000 transforms creative workflows with next-gen RTX capabilities, fusing AI with cutting-edge ray tracing for neural graphics. RTX Neural Shaders automate complex lighting and textures, while DLSS 4 enables real-time photorealistic rendering through AI-powered upscaling. These advancements accelerate 3D modeling, animation, and virtual production across film, gaming, and architectural visualization.
Learn More About NVIDIA RTX Technology
NVIDIA RTX Technology
Digital Content Creation

Accelerate content creation with real-time rendering and interactivity in 3D applications

Unlock your creative potential and bring your inspiring visions to life with RTX PRO 4000. Experience accelerated performance, real-time rendering, and AI-powered tools for higher fidelity visual quality and seamless workflow efficiency.
Accelerate content creation with real-time rendering and interactivity in 3D applications
Video Content and Streaming

Boost performance for live media and video pipelines.

The RTX PRO 4000 boosts performance for live media and video production pipelines. With 4:2:2 support and advanced encoding/decoding engines, it ensures pristine color accuracy and accelerated processing of high-resolution content. With improved AV1 and H.265 codec support, it s ideal for livestreaming, real-time editing, and live media workflows.
NVIDIA RTX PRO 4000 for Video Content and Streaming
Data Science

Accelerate your data pipelines.

RTX PRO 4000 gives you the performance you need to quickly transform massive datasets into insights with 24GB of ultra-fast G7 memory. RTX PRO 4000 combined with NVIDIA AI delivers a complete AI platform from desktop workstations.
Learn More About RTX PRO AI Workstations

Ways to Get Started With NVIDIA AI Enterprise Try for free through a web browser or via NVIDIA-hosted AI APIs. Download and prototype on your own infrastructure for free before deploying into production. Try Get started for free with NVIDIA-hosted NIM microservices for accelerated AI models and NVIDIA Blueprints for sample AI applications. Go to Free APIs Build Download AI and data science microservices, libraries, runtimes, and frameworks for free from NGC to develop and prototype applications on your own infrastructure. Explore Catalog Deploy When you re ready to run in production, get a license for NVIDIA AI Enterprise to run anywhere. The first 90 days are free. Request a Free 90-Day License

NVIDIA NIM API endpoints NVIDIA NeMo, DeepStream, NVIDIA Riva, and other SDKs and libraries NVIDIA tools for Kubernetes and virtualization support Downloadable NIM containers to self-host LLMs and multimodal AI models on preferred infrastructure * Production-grade software with API stability, regular security patching, and supply chain transparency
Try Now
Explore Catalog
Request Free 90-Day License
*requires free membership in NVIDIA Developer Program Resources For Enterprise AI Developers Explore, prototype, and test NVIDIA s latest accelerated AI APIs free. Build.nvidia.com gives developers hands-on access to serverless endpoints, blueprints, and GPUs with zero setup. Visit the API Catalog For IT and DevOps See all the supported infrastructure software designed to optimize the utilization of GPUs, and integrate with industry-leading management frameworks. Find a collection of reference architectures, deployment guides, whitepapers, and other informational resources in the NVIDIA AI Enterprise Documentation Hub. Explore Docs FAQs How can a developer get started quickly with NIM microservices? Members of the NVIDIA Developer Program have access to self-hosting NVIDIA NIM for research, application development, and experimentation on up to 16 GPUs on any infrastructure cloud, data center, or personal workstation. Access to NIM microservices is available throughout the duration of program membership. For more information, see this FAQ. What s the difference between open-source software and NVIDIA AI Enterprise? NVIDIA AI Enterprise consists of open-source software that s curated, optimized, and supported by NVIDIA, as well as exclusive software that isn t open source, such as NVIDIA NIM, NVIDIA Base Command Manager, and NVIDIA vGPU. It provides the benefits of transparency and top-of-tree innovation, as well as security and stability for ever-growing software dependencies. With enterprise support that provides service-level agreements, NVIDIA AI Enterprise is an enterprise-grade, secure, and reliable platform to accelerate your AI journey. How can I purchase NVIDIA AI Enterprise? You can purchase NVIDIA AI Enterprise through the NVIDIA Partner Network and the cloud marketplaces of AWS, Azure, Google Cloud, and Oracle Cloud. Contact NVIDIA with your purchasing questions. How does NVIDIA AI Enterprise deliver security and stability? NVIDIA AI Enterprise offers different software branches to maintain security and stability for desired software lifecycles, including: Feature branches: Every AI and data science package is updated monthly with the latest features and security fixes. It s meant for developers who want to deploy into production with the newest capabilities and are willing to update their applications if APIs change. Production branches: A nine-month lifecycle with monthly security patches and API stability. This is ideal for deploying AI in production when API stability is required. Long-term support branches: A three-year lifecycle with quarterly security patches and API stability. This is ideal for industries that prioritize long-term software lifetimes. NVIDIA AI Enterprise customers also receive security advisories and exploitability information from NVIDIA, as well as software bill of materials (SBOM) for software supply chain transparency, and vulnerability remediation guidance with context. Learn More What does Enterprise Support offer? Each NVIDIA AI Enterprise license includes Business Standard Support that offers service-level agreements, live NVIDIA agent access, 24/7 case filing, software branches, updates and releases, knowledge base resources, and more. Optional support upgrades, including Business Critical Support and Technical Account Manager, are available. Learn More What type of software is included with NVIDIA AI Enterprise? NVIDIA AI Enterprise includes NVIDIA NIM microservices, AI frameworks and runtimes, SDKs, libraries, infrastructure management, and more. View the full list here. How can a developer get started quickly with NIM microservices? Members of the NVIDIA Developer Program have access to self-hosting NVIDIA NIM for research, application development, and experimentation on up to 16 GPUs on any infrastructure cloud, data center, or personal workstation. Access to NIM microservices is available throughout the duration of program membership. For more information, see this FAQ. technical_terms.importance: Core technology enabling developers to run models on‑premises with NVIDIA support.

themes.description: The document emphasizes free access to APIs, catalog, and NIM microservices to enable rapid prototyping and evaluation before production.
themes.importance: Captures the overarching goal of lowering barriers to AI adoption.
themes.text: Accelerated AI development with free evaluation

pairs.answer: The workflow starts with joining the NVIDIA Developer Program, which grants access to self‑hosted NIM microservices on up to 16 GPUs for research and prototyping. Developers can then request a free 90‑day evaluation license for NVIDIA AI Enterprise, allowing them to test the platform and use its APIs, catalog, and microservices. During the evaluation they prototype applications using the available resources. Upon completing the evaluation, they can purchase a production license, which provides enterprise‑grade software with API stability, regular security patches, a supply‑chain bill of materials, and support options such as Business Standard or Critical Support, enabling deployment in production.

hop_contexts.summary: Describes the process to request a free 90‑day evaluation license for NVIDIA AI Enterprise.
Explains that NVIDIA Developer Program members can access self‑hosted NIM microservices for research and prototyping on up to 16 GPUs.
Details production‑grade software benefits (API stability, security patching, SBOM) and notes that a free membership in the Developer Program is required to obtain the evaluation license.

During the evaluation they prototype applications using the available resources. Upon completing the evaluation, they can purchase a production license, which provides enterprise‑grade software with API stability, regular security patches, a supply‑chain bill of materials, and support options such as Business Standard or Critical Support, enabling deployment in production.

FrameView App
FrameView is an application for measuring frame rates, frame times, power, and performance-per-watt on a wide range of graphics cards.
Download Now
Download Now
This application is not supported on mobile.
Close

Watch Video


Version 1.6 Release Notes
Reliable Performance and Power Data
Detailed metrics are presented in real-time with a configurable overlay, and reported to a log file for convenient analysis, leveraging PresentMon for analytics.
Extensive App and API Compatibility
Unlike other measurement options, FrameView works with a wide range of graphics cards, all major graphics APIs, and UWP (Universal Windows Platform) apps. Plus, it enables real-time power measurement through APIs, recording chip and board power* without the need for special physical equipment, while also capturing detailed performance information.
*Chip and board power on NVIDIA GPUs. Chip power only on AMD GPUs.
For more details, read our article and power primer, or download the user guide.
Download FrameView

This application is not supported on mobile.

Troubleshooting Tips
Streaming Non Gamestream Ready Games
GameStream officially supports over 300 games, but GeForce Experience allows users to manually add and stream nearly any game.
Open GeForce Experience and navigate to the Settings > SHIELD settings.

Under the Game and Apps window, click "ADD" and browse to the location of your game's launcher.

Add Games
(Optional) If you would like to edit the display and add custom box art to your manually added game, click "EDIT." Here you can set the display name by selecting the icon to edit the name and box art of the applications in your list.

Once you have added applications into the list, they will appear on your SHIELD and can be launched. Note that GameStream does not configure optimal settings for manually added games, so you may need to adjust in-game settings for the best experience.
